title: Can machine learning predict the liquidus temperature of binary alloys?
ml_models: Linear Regression, Random Forest, Deep Neural Network
target_properties: Liquidus temperature (T_L) of binary alloys
dataset_size: 85,523 alloy compositions from 429 binary alloy systems
features: Atomic size difference, atomic size range, average melting temperature, difference in melting temperature, heat of mixing, normalized heat of mixing, crystal structure mismatch
notes: Multiple feature sets tested: Physically Informed (PI, 7 features — listed), Elemental Properties (EP, 14 per alloy system), Composition Fractions (Comp, 2 features), and Random (benchmark). The 7 PI features are the primary reported set. Dataset extracted from phase diagrams using WebPlotDigitizer.

title: Role of dislocation locking and unlocking in the yield strength anomaly of γ-TiAl revealed by machine-learning moment tensor potential
ml_models: Moment Tensor Potential (MTP/MLIP)
target_properties: Interatomic energies and forces → dislocation core structures, locking/unlocking mechanisms in γ-TiAl
dataset_size: Not stated (DFT database cited as prior work)
features: Atomic positions and species; MTP moment tensor descriptors (no hand-crafted feature vector)
notes: MLIP model (MTP) trained on DFT database cited as reference [25]. Training database size not restated. The MTP is used as a surrogate for DFT to run MD/MS simulations — it is not a regression model predicting a scalar property from hand-crafted features.

title: Machine-learning prediction for solid–liquid interfacial properties from micrographs in Al–Cu and Fe–Mn alloys
ml_models: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), 2-output regression
target_properties: Solid-liquid interfacial free energy (γ₀), anisotropy parameter (ε)
dataset_size: 43,400 (Al-Cu) + 38,640 (Fe-Mn) phase-field simulation images
data_type: Computational (2D phase-field simulations; validated on experimental micrographs)
features: 256×256 binary microstructure images (raw pixel input)
notes: The CNN takes 256×256 binary images as raw input. Post-hoc analysis identifies 5 morphological descriptors (interfacial density, mean absolute curvature, spectral spacing, sidebranch power ratio, orientation coherence) as most informative for interpretation, but these are not used as model inputs. Dataset is purely computational; trained model is then applied to real experimental micrographs for validation.

title: Modulation of structural short-range order due to chemical patterning in multi-component amorphous interfacial complexions
ml_models: MACE ML-IAP (equivariant message-passing GNN)
target_properties: Interatomic energies and forces → dopant segregation, structural short-range order in Cu-Zr-Nb amorphous complexions
dataset_size: 8,692 DFT configurations
features: Atomic positions and species (Cu, Zr, Nb); MACE equivariant descriptors (no hand-crafted feature vector)
notes: MACE MLIP trained on Cu-Zr-Nb DFT configurations covering crystalline phases, solid solutions, and amorphous structures at 800–5000 K. RMSE: 5.4/5.9 meV/atom for energy. Used together with Monte Carlo simulations to study dopant segregation and structural SRO.

title: Data-selective machine learning framework (DSML) for defect-aware, interpretable yield-strength prediction for LPBF-fabricated AlSi10Mg alloys
ml_models: Ensemble Tree Regressor (ETR, best black-box); Symbolic Regression (PySR, white-box); also LASSO, LLAR, KNN, Ridge, Bayesian Ridge, Linear Regression
target_properties: Yield strength (σ_YS) of LPBF AlSi10Mg alloys; intermediate target: porosity
dataset_size: 155 total (44 fully labeled + 111 process-only)
features: Laser power (LP), scan speed (SS), layer thickness (LT), hatch spacing (HS), preheating temperature (T_pre), volumetric energy density (VED), cell size (d_cell), grain size (d_grain), porosity
notes: Two-stage framework: a porosity sub-model (5 process parameter features) feeds its output into the σ_YS model (8 or 9 features). Dataset 1 (44 samples) is fully labeled; Dataset 2 (111 samples) has only process params + porosity. Best black-box: ETR. Best white-box: Symbolic Regression (PySR), providing an interpretable equation.

title: Revealing cryo-loading induced twinning and its impact on titanium via correlative data-driven mechanomicroscopy
ml_models: Locally Weighted Linear Regression (LWLR); Ridge Regression (RR)
target_properties: Twin number per grain and twin area fraction (LWLR); nanoindentation hardness as function of grain orientation (RR)
dataset_size: ~8,000 twins from EBSD (378 twinned grains); ~30,000 nanoindentation data points
data_type: Experimental (EBSD + nanoindentation on cryogenically rolled pure Ti)
features: LWLR: grain size, maximum effective Schmid factor (SF_max); RR: Phi angle (c-axis to normal direction)
notes: Two separate ML models used for different sub-analyses: LWLR (grain size + Schmid factor → twin statistics) and Ridge Regression (Phi angle → nanoindentation hardness). The dataset is primarily statistical analysis of EBSD and nanoindentation maps, not a traditional train/test ML pipeline.

title: Local chemical inhomogeneities in Al-containing multi-principal element alloys explored by utilizing an ab-initio based approach
ml_models: LASSO Cluster Expansion (CE); Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering (AHC-Ward)
target_properties: Mixing enthalpy of BCC configurations (CE); local atomic environment dispersion/LAED as composition undulation descriptor (AHC)
dataset_size: 8,371 BCC DFT configs (4,454 retained after screening); 2,000-atom MC supercells
features: CE: cluster functions (2-body to 6-body interactions); AHC: local elemental concentrations of Ti, Al, Nb, Zr per atomic site
notes: Two distinct ML methods: (1) LASSO Cluster Expansion fitted to DFT energies for Ti-Al-Nb-Zr — features are cluster correlation functions; (2) Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering on local composition vectors from MC simulations. CE cluster count determined by LASSO regularization and is not explicitly reported.

title: Data-driven design of Nb-W refractory alloys using Transformer-based stress-strain modeling
ml_models: Multimodal Transformer Generative model for Stress-Strain curves (MTGSS)
target_properties: Full tensile stress-strain curves (up to 2% strain); derived: yield strength, UTS, elongation
dataset_size: 92 tensile stress-strain curves (8 literature + 84 experimental)
features: Alloy composition (elemental fractions) + thermomechanical processing conditions (encoded as Transformer tokens)
notes: The MTGSS model generates entire stress-strain curves (512 uniformly spaced points per curve) given alloy composition and processing conditions. This is a generative/sequence model, not a standard regressor. Only 92 training samples — very small dataset for a Transformer. Compositions include Nb, W, Mo, Zr, Hf, Ti, Ta, Si, V, Sc, Ru, B, Y, C, N.

title: Phase crossover enables large work output and low thermal hysteresis in shape memory actuation
ml_models: Ginzburg-Landau theory + finetuned surrogate model for transformation pathway mapping
target_properties: Work output, thermal hysteresis, martensitic transformation pathway (B2→B19′ vs B2→B19) in TiNiCuHfCo SMAs
dataset_size: ~7 compositions (Ti₅₀Ni₄₉.₃₋ₓCuₓHf₀.₃Co₀.₄, x=0,2,4,6,8,9,10)
data_type: Experimental (DSC, XRD, neutron diffraction, mechanical testing)
features: Composition fractions (Ginzburg-Landau order parameters guide feature engineering)
notes: Primarily a physics/experimental study using Ginzburg-Landau theory. The finetuned ML category refers to a previously trained surrogate model that was adapted. The ML component is secondary; the main contribution is identifying the B19'-B19 phase crossover as a mechanism for large work output and low hysteresis. Dataset is very small (~7 compositions).

title: Data-driven determination of the critical inclusion characteristic size in powder metallurgy superalloys
ml_models: Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) Neural Network; Physics-informed CICS-Transformer
target_properties: Critical inclusion characteristic size (CICS); low-cycle fatigue (LCF) life of PM Udimet 720Li
dataset_size: ~720 CPFE simulation samples
data_type: Mixed (experimental fatigue tests + crystal plasticity finite element (CPFE) simulations)
features: Physics-informed CPFE features (Δε_xx cyclic principal strain amplitude, f2, f3); inclusion parameters: size, shape, location, elastic mismatch
notes: Physics-Informed CICS-Transformer combines CPFE simulation outputs with a Transformer architecture for small-sample prediction. The physics-informed features (Δε_xx, f2, f3) are derived from CPFE simulations of each inclusion scenario. Exact total feature count not enumerated in the text.

title: Hydrogen diffusion in TiCr₂Hₓ Laves phases: A combined ab initio and machine-learning-potential study
ml_models: Moment Tensor Potentials (MTP), fine-tuned: C14-MTP and C15-MTP
target_properties: Hydrogen diffusion coefficients in C14 and C15 TiCr₂Hₓ Laves phases (x≤4); H migration barriers between interstitial sites
dataset_size: Not stated (pre-existing MTPs fine-tuned with additional DFT configurations)
features: Atomic positions and species (Ti, Cr, H); MTP moment tensor descriptors
notes: Two separate MTPs trained for C14 (hexagonal) and C15 (cubic) crystal structures, fine-tuned from pre-existing potentials (prior work [32]). Used to run long MD simulations for hydrogen diffusion — direct DFT MD would be too expensive. Training database size for fine-tuning is not stated.

title: Small features, big impact: Influence of overlooked microstructural features on high-entropy alloy predictions
ml_models: Gaussian Process Regression (GPR); Support Vector Regression (SVR)
target_properties: Vickers hardness of AlCrFeNiTi high-entropy alloys
dataset_size: ~203 total (~181 without nano-precipitates)
features: Al, Cr, Fe, Ni, Ti elemental composition fractions
notes: Key finding: nano-precipitate presence (a microstructural feature not captured by composition) significantly degrades ML prediction accuracy. The model uses only 5 compositional features, which is why nano-precipitate effects are overlooked. Dataset of ~203 points is split by presence/absence of nano-precipitates.

title: Predicting Interstitial Solutes in Refractory Complex Concentrated Alloys via a Combined Experimental and Computational Workflow: A Case Study on Oxygen in NbTaTiHf
ml_models: ORB-v2 universal MLIP (GNN, fine-tuned foundation model); Bayesian optimization
target_properties: Formation energies and site preferences of interstitial oxygen (O) in NbTaTiHf RCCAs; O segregation to grain boundaries
dataset_size: 330 DFT structures (165 alloy + 165 alloy+O)
data_type: Mixed (experimental casting/characterization + DFT validation + MLIP simulations)
features: Crystal structure graphs (atomic positions, species); GNN learned representations (no hand-crafted feature vector)
notes: ORB-v2 is a universal GNN-based MLIP (foundation model from OrbitMaterials) fine-tuned for the NbTaTiHf+O system. The workflow combines experimental alloy casting, DFT validation, and MLIP-based simulations. Bayesian optimization finds compositions that minimize O segregation to grain boundaries.

title: Liquid metal embrittlement in Fe-Zn: What can we learn from atomistic simulations?
ml_models: Atomic Cluster Expansion (ACE) potential + SpinACE (magnetic effects), fitted with PACEMAKER; active learning with hyperactive learning
target_properties: Interatomic energies and forces in Fe-Zn → fracture mechanics, grain boundary embrittlement, liquid metal embrittlement
dataset_size: 11,018+ configurations (enriched Fe Meng database + Zn + Fe-Zn DFT calculations)
features: ACE polynomial basis functions of atomic positions, species, and magnetic moments
notes: SpinACE extends the standard ACE potential to include magnetic degrees of freedom, critical for Fe (ferromagnetic). Training database combines the Fe Meng database with new Zn and Fe-Zn DFT configurations. Active learning (hyperactive learning) used to fill gaps in the training database. Validated against DFT-calculated surface energies, grain boundary energies, and fracture mechanics.

title: Dual engine-driven strategy for advanced copper alloy design employing large language models
ml_models: Deep Neural Network (DNN), LSTM, LSTM-NN, attention-based NN (data engine); LLM/GPT (knowledge engine)
target_properties: Hardness and electrical conductivity (EC) of Cu-Ni-Sn alloys
dataset_size: 234,361 alloy entries (AFLOW/Materials Project database) + ~24 experimental validations; LLM processed 10,305 articles
data_type: Experimental (literature database + LLM-mined text + new experimental validation)
features: 14 features for hardness; 16 features for EC — selected from 96 generated features (chemical/physical elemental properties: quantum numbers, atomic masses, etc.)
notes: Dual-engine framework: data engine uses 234,361 alloy entries; knowledge engine (LLM/GPT) processes 10,305 copper alloy research articles for mechanism-level insights. Feature selection from 96 generated features down to 14 (hardness) and 16 (EC) using correlation analysis. Exact selected feature names not enumerated in the text.

title: Dislocation-mediated short-range order evolution during thermomechanical processing
ml_models: ML Interatomic Potential (GNN-based, NequIP-type) for equiatomic TiTaVW
target_properties: Interatomic energies and forces in TiTaVW → SRO evolution under dislocation-mediated thermomechanical processing
dataset_size: Not stated (DFT configurations of perfect and defective TiTaVW structures)
features: Atomic positions and species (Ti, Ta, V, W); GNN descriptors (no hand-crafted feature vector)
notes: The MLIP (likely NequIP or similar equivariant GNN) is used to run large-scale MD simulations of dislocation motion and SRO evolution in equiatomic TiTaVW, inaccessible by direct DFT. Training database size not stated. The study reveals how dislocation motion creates local chemical order variations.

title: Interfacial atomic vibration: A key dynamic characteristic for heterogeneous nucleation substrates
ml_models: Machine Learning Force Field (MLFF, on-the-fly via VASP 6.4.2, Bayesian inference)
target_properties: Interfacial atomic vibrational properties (vibrational spectra, mean square displacement); heterogeneous nucleation efficiency
dataset_size: Not stated (AIMD on-the-fly training trajectories)
data_type: Computational (DFT/AIMD + MLFF MD; experimental grain refinement validation)
features: Atomic positions and species (no hand-crafted features; on-the-fly VASP MLFF)
notes: On-the-fly MLFF training within VASP 6.4.2 using Bayesian inference. The MLFF is trained adaptively during AIMD simulation — when prediction uncertainty exceeds a threshold, new DFT calculations are added to the training set. Total training set size is therefore not a fixed number.

title: Design of shape memory alloys with enhanced thermal management properties via adaptively constrained multi-objective optimization
ml_models: Gradient Boosting (feature selection + ranking); surrogate regression model; Bayesian optimization / Expected Hypervolume Improvement (EHVI) multi-objective optimization
target_properties: Latent heat (L) for thermal energy storage; transformation hysteresis (ΔT) for overall transformation range in NiTi-based SMAs
dataset_size: 125 NiTi-based alloy samples
data_type: Experimental (DSC data from authors' lab + literature)
features: Top 10 selected from 31 candidate features: compositions of Ti, Ni, Cu, Hf, Zr, Nb, Co, Cr, Fe, V + derived thermodynamic features
notes: Two-step feature selection: Gradient Boosting ranks all 31 candidate features, then Maximal Information Coefficient (MIC) removes redundant ones. Bayesian optimization with EHVI targets the Pareto front of latent heat vs. transformation hysteresis. Dataset of 125 samples spans NiTi-based alloys with up to 10 alloying elements.

title: High-throughput design of Mg-based alloy anodes for magnesium-ion batteries
ml_models: ML pre-screening model (architecture unspecified); multi-criteria DFT stability validation
target_properties: Theoretical specific capacity, thermodynamic stability (formation energy), electronic conductivity, anti-passivation, Mg affinity, diffusion barriers of Mg-based alloy anodes
dataset_size: ~613 screened → 168 stable → 107 further evaluated
features: Alloy composition (Mg fraction x + partner element); crystal structure descriptors (not explicitly detailed)
notes: ML pre-screening is used as a first filter over a large compositional space before expensive DFT validation. The exact ML model architecture is not explicitly stated in the text. The screening pipeline evaluates six criteria: specific capacity, stability, conductivity, anti-passivation, Mg affinity, and diffusion barriers.

title: Atomic-scale insights into the exceptional pinning effect of the K bubbles on W grain boundary
ml_models: Neuroevolution Potential (NEP / WK-NEP) via GPUMD
target_properties: Interatomic energies and forces in W-K → grain boundary migration, K bubble pinning, GB energy and mobility in W
dataset_size: Not stated (unary W, K + binary W-K DFT configurations)
features: Atomic positions and species (W, K); Chebyshev polynomial NEP descriptors
notes: NEP uses a neural network with Chebyshev polynomial basis for atomic density descriptors, trained via GPUMD. Used for large-scale MD simulations of GB migration with K bubbles — system sizes up to millions of atoms inaccessible by DFT. Training database covers unary W, K, and binary W-K configurations at various temperatures.

title: Towards DFT-accurate prediction of twin interface structure and motion in NiTi shape memory alloys
ml_models: Atomic Cluster Expansion (ACE) potential, trained with PACEMAKER; multiple ACE models compared
target_properties: Interatomic energies and forces in NiTi → twin interface structure, twin boundary energy, twin boundary mobility in B19′ martensite
dataset_size: 10,626 NiTi configurations (AIMD: B2, B19′, B19, B33, R phases + off-stoichiometric + active learning structures)
features: ACE polynomial many-body basis (atomic positions, species Ni/Ti)
notes: Multiple ACE variants compared (ACE-1, ACE-2, etc.) to find the optimal accuracy/cost balance. Training database of 10,626 configurations from Tang et al. [35] covers B2, B19', B19, B33, R phases, off-stoichiometric structures, and active learning configurations. The ACE potential achieves DFT-level accuracy for twin boundary energy calculations.

title: Uncertainty-aware machine learning framework for predicting dislocation plasticity and stress–strain response in metallic alloys, Part I: FCC systems
ml_models: Mixture Density Network (MDN) + parallel-series weakest-link micromechanics framework
target_properties: Dislocation density evolution as function of strain ρ(ε); polycrystalline stress-strain curve with uncertainty quantification
dataset_size: Literature dataset of quasi-static tensile stress-strain curves for FCC metals (exact count not stated)
data_type: Experimental (compiled from literature: quasi-static tensile tests)
features: True strain (ε), strain rate (ε̇), average grain size (d_ave), shear modulus (μ), yield strength (σ_y)
notes: MDN outputs a probability distribution over dislocation density evolution — provides uncertainty quantification. Combined with a physics-based parallel-series weakest-link micromechanics model to predict full polycrystalline stress-strain curves. The 5 input features are physically meaningful. Exact count of training stress-strain curves from literature not stated.

title: Probing dendrite growth under microgravity via machine learning-aided multi-scale characterisation
ml_models: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) + bespoke image analysis
target_properties: Dendrite growth kinetics (primary arm evolution, growth rate, orientation selection) under microgravity vs. terrestrial conditions
dataset_size: X-ray image sequences from MASER-13 rocket experiment (exact frame count not stated)
data_type: Experimental (in-situ X-ray radiography during microgravity solidification + post-flight synchrotron X-ray micro-tomography)
features: Raw X-ray radiography image pixels (CNN input); no explicit hand-crafted feature vector
notes: CNN applied to in-situ X-ray radiography images from the MASER-13 sounding rocket experiment. The ML component automates dendrite morphology analysis. The dataset is unique and rare (microgravity experiment). Post-flight synchrotron X-ray micro-tomography provides 3D structural characterization for comparison.

title: Theory guided data-driven design of superelasticity in TiNi alloys over wide temperature window
ml_models: Gaussian Process Regression (GPR); Symbolic Regression; theory-guided features from Ginzburg-Landau/Landau mean-field theory
target_properties: Superelastic temperature window (ΔT from -196°C to 100°C) in TiNi-based alloys; transformation temperatures (M_s, A_f)
dataset_size: 185 data points (literature + authors' lab; TiNi-based alloys)
data_type: Experimental (literature + lab measurements; theory-guided feature engineering)
features: Alloy composition fractions (Ti, Ni + dopants); Landau-derived order parameter features (theory-informed descriptors)
notes: Key innovation: Ginzburg-Landau/Landau mean-field theory used to define theory-guided features encoding the superelastic window physics. GPR is the surrogate model; Symbolic Regression extracts interpretable equations. Dataset of 185 TiNi-based alloy samples from literature and lab. Exact feature names not fully enumerated in available text.

title: Tailoring thermal transport in (Sc,Yb)AlN thin films to the glassy limit
ml_models: Neuroevolution Potential (NEP) via GPUMD; Debye-Callaway (D-C) analytical model; Cahill-Watson-Pohl model
target_properties: Thermal conductivity (k) of (Sc,Yb)AlN thin films as function of alloy composition and structural disorder
dataset_size: DFT on 256-atom SQS supercells for various Sc/Yb concentrations (training set size not stated)
data_type: Mixed (experimental RF sputtering of thin films + DFT + NEP/MD simulations)
features: Atomic positions and species (Sc/Yb, Al, N); alloy concentration x as primary design variable
notes: NEP trained on DFT calculations of Special Quasi-random Structures (SQS) — 256-atom supercells at various Sc/Yb concentrations. NEP enables HNEMD and QHGK calculations of thermal conductivity. Training set size not explicitly stated. Three models compared: NEP/MD, Debye-Callaway, and Cahill-Watson-Pohl.

title: Design biomedical β-Ti alloys with exceptional strength-ductility balance via domain knowledge-based machine learning
ml_models: Support Vector Regression (SVR) + SHAP; also Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Neural Network; Bayesian Optimization for composition selection
target_properties: Yield strength and elongation (ductility) of biomedical β-Ti alloys
dataset_size: ~82 data points (literature; inferred from context)
features: Alloy composition fractions (Ti + alloying elements) + domain knowledge features related to slip systems and kink band formation
notes: SVR is the primary model with SHAP for interpretability. Domain knowledge encoded via features related to slip systems and kink band formation mechanisms specific to β-Ti alloys. Dataset size (~82) inferred from context — not explicitly stated. Bayesian Optimization suggests new compositions on the Pareto front of yield strength vs. elongation.

title: Creating Gallium-based alloys with the lowest melting point via reverse design approach
ml_models: Neural Network, LASSO, Ridge Regression, Gradient Boosting, SVM, Gaussian Process; active learning for inverse design
target_properties: Melting point of Gallium-based low-melting-point alloys (goal: minimize below current record)
dataset_size: 150 literature samples (19 metal combinations: Ga, In, Sn, Zn, Bi, Pb, Cd, Li, Al, Na, Gd, Ge, Tl, Ti, Ag, La, Cu, Sb, Co)
features: 128 descriptors: 109 atomic descriptors + 19 elemental ratio descriptors (quantum numbers, atomic masses, group numbers, etc.)
num_features: 128 initial → reduced via feature selection (final count not stated)
notes: Active learning inverse design: ML model trained on 150 literature samples then iteratively proposes new compositions to test. 128 initial descriptors generated from element properties for 19 possible metals. Feature selection reduces this, but the final count is not stated. Six ML models compared; best used for active learning.

title: Microstructure-agnostic deep learning for mechanistic discovery of corrosion-resistant Co-Cr-Fe-Ni MPEAs
ml_models: CORAL (Deep Neural Network with dropout); Gradient Boost, AdaBoost, Kernel Ridge Regression, Gaussian Process; Random Forest (feature importance); active learning
target_properties: Corrosion current density (i_corr) of Co-Cr-Fe-Ni multi-principal element alloys in 0.5 M H₂SO₄
dataset_size: ~1,400 literature data points + 35 new experimental = ~1,435 total
data_type: Mixed (literature experimental data + new experiments + DFT calculations for descriptor development)
features: Physics-based descriptors (composition + DFT-derived invariant features; grain boundary/secondary phase concepts at design level)
notes: CORAL is a multi-layer DNN with dropout for uncertainty analysis. Key innovation: microstructure-agnostic approach uses physics-based descriptors invariant to microstructural variation. Feature importance ranked by 5-fold Random Forest. Active learning adds targeted experimental points to improve the model in underrepresented compositional regions.

title: Prediction and characterization of chemically complex σ-phase intermetallics with graph neural network
ml_models: ALIGNN (Atomistic Line Graph Neural Network — dual-graph GNN); Bayesian optimization for hyperparameter tuning
target_properties: Formation enthalpies of σ-phase intermetallic end-member configurations for 19 metallic elements
dataset_size: ~3,000 DFT-calculated σ-phase configurations (custom first-principles database)
features: Crystal structure dual-graph: bond distances and bond angles (ALIGNN input; no hand-crafted feature vector)
notes: ALIGNN uses a dual-graph representation: one graph for atomic bonds and a line graph encoding bond angles, capturing 3-body interactions without hand-crafted angular features. Applied to predict formation enthalpies of all end-member configurations in complex σ-phase intermetallics (19 elements). Bayesian optimization used for hyperparameter tuning. ~3,000 DFT training configurations.
